verses.text: Two sisters, Aholah (Samaria) and Aholibah (Jerusalem), played the harlot from their youth in Egypt. Shared corruption. The two kingdoms had a common heritage of unfaithfulness.
Two sisters, Aholah (Samaria) and Aholibah (Jerusalem), played the harlot from their youth in Egypt. Shared corruption. The two kingdoms had a common heritage of unfaithfulness.
Two sisters, Aholah (Samaria) and Aholibah (Jerusalem), played the harlot from their youth in Egypt. Shared corruption. The two kingdoms had a common heritage of unfaithfulness.
Two sisters, Aholah (Samaria) and Aholibah (Jerusalem), played the harlot from their youth in Egypt. Shared corruption. The two kingdoms had a common heritage of unfaithfulness.
Aholah doted on the Assyrians, defiling herself with their idols. Misplaced love. Samaria chased foreign powers and their gods instead of trusting the LORD.
Aholah doted on the Assyrians, defiling herself with their idols. Misplaced love. Samaria chased foreign powers and their gods instead of trusting the LORD.
Aholah doted on the Assyrians, defiling herself with their idols. Misplaced love. Samaria chased foreign powers and their gods instead of trusting the LORD.
Aholah doted on the Assyrians, defiling herself with their idols. Misplaced love. Samaria chased foreign powers and their gods instead of trusting the LORD.
So God gave her into Assyrian hands, who exposed and slew her. Sin's wage. The lovers she pursued became her destroyers.
So God gave her into Assyrian hands, who exposed and slew her. Sin's wage. The lovers she pursued became her destroyers.
Her sister Aholibah saw all this yet was 'more corrupt... than she.' Worse for the warning. Jerusalem sinned against the example of judgment before her eyes.
Her sister Aholibah saw all this yet was 'more corrupt... than she.' Worse for the warning. Jerusalem sinned against the example of judgment before her eyes.
Her sister Aholibah saw all this yet was 'more corrupt... than she.' Worse for the warning. Jerusalem sinned against the example of judgment before her eyes.
She doted on the Chaldeans she saw portrayed, sending for them to Babylon. Lust kindled by sight. The imagination feeds an idolatry the heart already craves.
She doted on the Chaldeans she saw portrayed, sending for them to Babylon. Lust kindled by sight. The imagination feeds an idolatry the heart already craves.
She doted on the Chaldeans she saw portrayed, sending for them to Babylon. Lust kindled by sight. The imagination feeds an idolatry the heart already craves.
The Babylonians defiled her, then her soul was alienated from them. Sin's disgust. Pursued pleasures soon breed loathing, yet not repentance.
The Babylonians defiled her, then her soul was alienated from them. Sin's disgust. Pursued pleasures soon breed loathing, yet not repentance.
Still she multiplied her whoredoms, recalling the sins of her youth in Egypt. Old sins revived. The unrenewed heart returns to its earliest idols.
Still she multiplied her whoredoms, recalling the sins of her youth in Egypt. Old sins revived. The unrenewed heart returns to its earliest idols.
Still she multiplied her whoredoms, recalling the sins of her youth in Egypt. Old sins revived. The unrenewed heart returns to its earliest idols.
Therefore God will raise her former lovers against her, the Babylonians coming with weapons. Lovers turned foes. The nations she trusted become the rod of her judgment.
Therefore God will raise her former lovers against her, the Babylonians coming with weapons. Lovers turned foes. The nations she trusted become the rod of her judgment.
Therefore God will raise her former lovers against her, the Babylonians coming with weapons. Lovers turned foes. The nations she trusted become the rod of her judgment.
He will set His jealousy against her, deal furiously, and 'make thy lewdness to cease.' Judgment ends the sin. God's severity at last cuts off what mercy could not cure.
He will set His jealousy against her, deal furiously, and 'make thy lewdness to cease.' Judgment ends the sin. God's severity at last cuts off what mercy could not cure.
He will set His jealousy against her, deal furiously, and 'make thy lewdness to cease.' Judgment ends the sin. God's severity at last cuts off what mercy could not cure.
He will deliver her to those she hates, because she went 'a whoring after the heathen.' Just recompense. The friendships she chose against God become her ruin.
He will deliver her to those she hates, because she went 'a whoring after the heathen.' Just recompense. The friendships she chose against God become her ruin.
He will deliver her to those she hates, because she went 'a whoring after the heathen.' Just recompense. The friendships she chose against God become her ruin.
She walks her sister's path and shall drink her cup deep, filled with 'drunkenness and sorrow.' The same cup. Like sin reaps like judgment.
She walks her sister's path and shall drink her cup deep, filled with 'drunkenness and sorrow.' The same cup. Like sin reaps like judgment.
She walks her sister's path and shall drink her cup deep, filled with 'drunkenness and sorrow.' The same cup. Like sin reaps like judgment.
She walks her sister's path and shall drink her cup deep, filled with 'drunkenness and sorrow.' The same cup. Like sin reaps like judgment.
Because she forgot God, she must bear her lewdness; both sisters are adulteresses 'with blood... in their hands.' Forgetting God. Idolatry and bloodshed go together.
Because she forgot God, she must bear her lewdness; both sisters are adulteresses 'with blood... in their hands.' Forgetting God. Idolatry and bloodshed go together.
Because she forgot God, she must bear her lewdness; both sisters are adulteresses 'with blood... in their hands.' Forgetting God. Idolatry and bloodshed go together.
They defiled His sanctuary, profaned His sabbaths, and slew their children for idols, then came into His house. Brazen hypocrisy. They mingled murder with worship without shame.
They defiled His sanctuary, profaned His sabbaths, and slew their children for idols, then came into His house. Brazen hypocrisy. They mingled murder with worship without shame.
They sent for men from afar, decked themselves, and set God's oil and incense on a stately couch. Sacred things abused. They lavished holy gifts on their idolatrous feasts.
They sent for men from afar, decked themselves, and set God's oil and incense on a stately couch. Sacred things abused. They lavished holy gifts on their idolatrous feasts.
They sent for men from afar, decked themselves, and set God's oil and incense on a stately couch. Sacred things abused. They lavished holy gifts on their idolatrous feasts.
God asks if the worn adulteress will yet sin on; righteous men shall judge them as adulteresses. Verdict pronounced. Persistent sin meets a righteous sentence.
God asks if the worn adulteress will yet sin on; righteous men shall judge them as adulteresses. Verdict pronounced. Persistent sin meets a righteous sentence.
God asks if the worn adulteress will yet sin on; righteous men shall judge them as adulteresses. Verdict pronounced. Persistent sin meets a righteous sentence.
He will bring a company against them to spoil them, that they may 'bear the sins of your idols, and know that I am the Lord GOD.' Sin's burden. Judgment teaches at last the lesson mercy could not.
He will bring a company against them to spoil them, that they may 'bear the sins of your idols, and know that I am the Lord GOD.' Sin's burden. Judgment teaches at last the lesson mercy could not.
